title: Licence/provenance/PII + sensitivity gate (CC-BY-IGO)
context: Every derivative dataset and brief must be built from a source confirmed openly licensed for derivatives under CC BY 4.0 IGO, with FAO attribution and non-endorsement disclaimer recorded. FAOSTAT is not uniformly CC-BY-IGO across all domains — some incorporate third-party data with their own terms. Additionally, the gate must screen for PII (aggregate-only policy) and exclude acute-crisis/famine framing and advice-adjacent topics. A formal, reusable gate specification is needed so every topic passes the same non-skippable check before any work begins.
objective: Design and document the licence/provenance/PII and sensitivity gate that must be applied to every indicator before a derivative dataset or brief is created.
acceptanceCriteria: Verifies that the indicator's source licence is CC BY 4.0 IGO (or equivalent open derivative-permitting licence), records permitsDerivatives: true with a cited clause and URL, and records the attribution string and non-endorsement disclaimer.
Per-indicator third-party-data check: excludes or escalates any FAOSTAT domain whose underlying data is not uniformly CC-BY-IGO, with explicit FLAG/EXCLUDE and surfaced concern.
PII screen: confirms pii.level is aggregate; hard-excludes microdata, household-survey, or re-identifiable data.
Sensitivity screen: excludes acute-crisis/famine framing (IPC, Cadre Harmonisé) and any advice-adjacent topic; flags anything requiring escalation to credentialed expert sign-off.
Records licence id, URL, and snapshot reference (committed copy + SHA-256 + Wayback Machine save URL) per indicator.
Produces a committed PASS/FLAG/EXCLUDE artifact per topic with full rationale; any unresolved FLAG or EXCLUDE blocks the topic from proceeding.
Output licensed CC-BY-4.0; commit is DCO signed-off.
resources: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International IGO (CC BY 4.0 IGO)
output: A committed gate specification document and per-indicator gate-artifact template (PASS/FLAG/EXCLUDE), ready for use by every brief and dataset task.
